task: Locate the code path that registers VBlank chain handler 0xBFC425BC

proof_registrar_synthesizes_handler.uniqueness: only instance in entire 512KB ROM of 'addiu *,*,0xA5BC' is at 0xBFC4254C

registrar_body_semantics.note_0xB005A090: RAM-side kernel routine (shell-copy region). normalize() in SCPH1001_dispatch.c maps phys 0x5A090 -> 0x1FC42090, which IS in dispatch table as func_1FC42090. So call is reachable by dispatcher.

static_callers_audit.method: scanned every 4-byte-aligned J-format (jal/j) in ROM for target-match on both ROM (0xBFCxxxxx) and RAM (0x8005xxxx / 0xB005xxxx) aliases

synthesis_audit.method: searched for lui/addiu (or lui/ori) pairs within 16 instructions that reconstruct each candidate address
synthesis_audit.synthesizers_of_0x8005A5FC: 1 (only the registrar's own return-value code at 0xBFC425AC-0xBFC425B0)

contents.+0x0C: 0x8005A160  (func_1FC42160 — in dispatch table, kernel init that calls registrar)

irq_descriptor_table_candidate.interpretation: callback descriptor for intr.c (root counter IRQ) subsystem. Three entries likely = {dispatch-on-IRQ, init, cleanup}. Walking this table is how the kernel hooks the VBlank chain.

cross_reference_audit.conclusion: No statically-resolvable code path references 0xBFC61D00..0xBFC61D20. Table is either accessed via a pointer-to-pointer indirection (gp-relative, register-loaded from an earlier data struct, or sum-of-two-registers), OR the walker function itself is inside a region we have not yet discovered/seeded.

additional_negative_results_2026_04_13_l.final_verdict: No 16-bit immediate in the entire 512KB ROM matches the low-half of any address in the candidate table region. Table is provably unreachable by any standard lui+addiu, lui+ori, or lui-carry pattern. Either the table is dead data, or it is reached via pointer-indirection loaded from elsewhere in memory at runtime (only discoverable via dynamic trace).

remaining_unknown.question: What function (or data-driven init path) walks the callback descriptor table at 0xBFC61D00 and installs 0xBFC42160 / 0xBFC42540 into the active IRQ chain?
why_static_analysis_stopped: zero references (lui/ori, lui/addiu, or 4-byte literal) to the candidate table at 0xBFC61D00
the only 8 'lui *,0xBFC6' sites in ROM all resolve to 0xBFC66000 or 0xBFC69D68, never 0xBFC61xxx
per Rule 14 (Unknown is acceptable. Guessing is not.) — stopping static search here

next_step_per_handoff_plan_step_1.method_a: execute-breakpoint at 0xBFC425BC; when it fires, dump call stack; outer frame identifies the caller
next_step_per_handoff_plan_step_1.method_b: memory-read watchpoint on 0x80079D9C (VSync counter); when it fires inside handler, step back to find incoming $ra, then find what enqueued the handler onto the chain

observed.evcb_0x8000E028_to_0x8000E1F0: zero-filled by InitEvent(16) at seq 42-57; no class-0xF2xxxxxx writes observed across 12s sample

runtime_state.implication: registrar 0xBFC42540 is seeded and callable by the dispatcher, but nothing in the running code actually reaches the `jal 0xB005A540` instruction at either 0xBFC42340 or 0xBFC42604.

tools_added_this_session: tools/find_callers.py   — find all ROM PCs that JAL/J to a given target
tools/find_const.py     — find all lui/addiu pairs synthesizing a given 32-bit constant
tools/scan_literal.py   — find 4-byte literal occurrences of an address in ROM
tools/scan_range.py     — find pointers within a given address range anywhere in ROM
tools/scan_lui.py       — list all 'lui *,X' instructions in ROM
